ai_research_audit.reviewer_identity: OpenAI Codex GPT-5 research-audit agent (AI, non-clinician, no medical credentials)
ai_research_audit.summary: The evidence package is internally sufficient as a conservative specification for implementation and synthetic testing. It is not clinical approval or authorization for real-user or production use. Exact catalog coverage remains provisional because its input artifacts are not pinned to the reviewed commit.
exceptions: No real-user, external-beta, or production use
Preserve all referral, abstention, contraindication, and unknown-input deferrals
Pin or regenerate catalog coverage inputs before relying on exact counts or SKUs

sources.title: Guidelines of care for the management of acne vulgaris
sources.publisher: Journal of the American Academy of Dermatology / PubMed
sources.recommendation_strength: Strong recommendations for benzoyl peroxide and topical retinoids; conditional recommendations for salicylic acid and azelaic acid; strong isotretinoin recommendation in specified severe or refractory contexts.
sources.exact_excerpt: Strong recommendations are made for benzoyl peroxide, topical retinoids, topical antibiotics, and oral doxycycline.
sources.conclusion: Supports mechanism-specific acne therapy and escalation for severe, scarring, burdensome, or refractory disease.
sources.uncertainty: The accessible abstract does not provide every operational strength, cadence, or population detail; regulatory labels and NICE supply bounded operational facts.

sources.title: Acne vulgaris: management — Recommendations (NG198)
sources.publisher: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.recommendation_strength: Current national clinical-practice recommendations, including first-line 12-week courses, do-not-use antibiotic monotherapy rules, referral criteria, pregnancy exclusions, and scar referral.
sources.exact_excerpt: Offer people with acne a 12-week course of 1 of the following first-line treatment options
sources.conclusion: Supplies operational care duration, combination strengths, review timing, referral criteria, and safety constraints.
sources.uncertainty: UK treatment availability and care pathways do not automatically establish US product availability or labeling.

sources.title: OTC Monograph M006: Topical Acne Drug Products for OTC Human Use
sources.recommendation_strength: Legally operative US OTC monograph conditions, not a comparative clinical recommendation.
sources.conclusion: Establishes permitted OTC acne-drug concentrations for benzoyl peroxide 2.5%-10% and salicylic acid 0.5%-2% and the applicable Drug Facts framework.
sources.uncertainty: Does not determine which eligible active is best for a particular person or detector label.

sources.title: Adapalene gel 0.1% Drug Facts label
sources.publisher: US National Library of Medicine DailyMed (FDA-submitted label)
sources.recommendation_strength: Product-specific FDA labeling, not comparative efficacy guidance.
sources.exact_excerpt: Adults and children 12 years of age and older: use once daily
sources.conclusion: Supports exact adapalene 0.1% identity, acne indication, age 12+ boundary, once-daily labeled use, and irritation/pregnancy consultation warnings.
sources.uncertainty: Does not authorize application-generated directions or settle nursing eligibility.

sources.title: Skin Conditions During Pregnancy
sources.publisher: American College of Obstetricians and Gynecologists
sources.exact_excerpt: it is generally recommended that use of these medications be avoided during pregnancy.
sources.conclusion: Supports pregnancy-specific avoidance of topical retinoids and identifies pregnancy-compatible topical ingredients for clinician-reviewed policy wording.
sources.uncertainty: Does not provide product-specific dosing and does not replace obstetric consultation.

sources.title: Acne: Signs and symptoms
sources.exact_excerpt: A whitehead forms when excess oil and dead skin cells build up and plug the opening of a pore.
sources.conclusion: Provides plain-language distinctions among whiteheads, blackheads, papules, pustules, nodules, and cysts.
sources.uncertainty: Does not validate photo-based classification or diagnosis.

sources.title: Is that stubborn acne really acne?
sources.exact_excerpt: Other skin conditions can look a lot like acne.
sources.conclusion: Documents acne lookalikes and the role of dermatologist diagnosis.
sources.uncertainty: Not a systematic differential-diagnosis review and not a model-validation study.

sources.title: Acne scars: Consultation and treatment
sources.exact_excerpt: Stopping acne breakouts can reduce inflammation and prevent new acne scars.
sources.conclusion: Supports controlling active acne, clinician scar-subtype assessment, and individualized depressed-scar procedures; selected mild scars may be discussed for a topical retinoid or salicylic acid.
sources.uncertainty: No single procedure, exact regimen, or effect size applies to all scar subtypes.

sources.title: Scars: Diagnosis and treatment
sources.exact_excerpt: Silicone gel (sheet or ointment): Used since 1982, silicone can: Treat a raised scar
sources.conclusion: Supports clinician confirmation of raised-scar type and conditional silicone sheet or ointment use only on closed skin, often for months, with rash or breakdown as stop signals.
sources.uncertainty: Does not establish high-certainty comparative efficacy or an exact retail product specification.

sources.title: Silicone gel sheeting for treating hypertrophic scars
sources.publisher: Cochrane Database of Systematic Reviews / PubMed
sources.recommendation_strength: Systematic review finding sparse, generally very-low-certainty randomized evidence.
sources.exact_excerpt: it is uncertain whether there is a difference in severity of scarring between the two groups
sources.conclusion: Does not support a confident automated silicone recommendation.
sources.uncertainty: Few small trials, limited comparisons, and very low certainty for most outcomes.

sources.title: Melasma: Diagnosis and treatment
sources.recommendation_strength: Professional-association diagnosis, photoprotection, and treatment guidance.
sources.exact_excerpt: For patients who have melasma, dermatologists often recommend using a sunscreen that contains: Zinc oxide Titanium dioxide Iron oxide
sources.conclusion: Supports diagnostic examination, broad-spectrum SPF 30+ and iron-oxide visible-light protection, clinician-directed pigment therapy, and a 3-12 month improvement horizon.
sources.uncertainty: Does not specify an optimal iron-oxide concentration or authorize diagnosis from a photograph.

sources.title: Topical Treatments for Melasma and Their Mechanism of Action
sources.publisher: Journal of Clinical and Aesthetic Dermatology / PubMed
sources.recommendation_strength: Systematic review of 36 studies and 47 comparisons; evidence quality varied from very low to high.
sources.exact_excerpt: Evidence suggests sunscreen covering the ultraviolet (UV)- and visible light spectra increases treatment efficacy compared with UV-only protection.
sources.conclusion: Supports triple combination, hydroquinone, tretinoin, and visible-light-protective sunscreen as evidence-bearing options.
sources.uncertainty: Small heterogeneous trials, varied outcomes, and wide certainty range limit automatic regimen selection.

sources.title: FDA works to protect consumers from potentially harmful OTC skin lightening products
sources.recommendation_strength: US regulatory status and safety communication.
sources.exact_excerpt: there are no FDA-approved or otherwise legally marketed OTC skin lightening products.
sources.conclusion: Supports blocking OTC hydroquinone skin-lightening products and treating hydroquinone as clinician-directed prescription therapy in the US.
sources.uncertainty: US-specific regulatory conclusion; does not compare clinician-prescribed therapies.

sources.title: Moles: Diagnosis and treatment
sources.exact_excerpt: Never try to remove a mole at home
sources.conclusion: Supports no treatment for most stable moles, clinician examination/removal when suspicious or bothersome, microscopy, and no home removal.
sources.uncertainty: Does not validate automated benign-versus-malignant classification.

sources.title: Moles: Signs and symptoms
sources.exact_excerpt: If you see a mole or new spot on your skin that has any of the ABCDEs of melanoma
sources.conclusion: Supports ABCDE change wording and prompt review for concerning features.
sources.uncertainty: ABCDE does not identify every melanoma and cannot be reduced to a model-confidence threshold.

sources.title: When is a mole a problem?
sources.exact_excerpt: If a mole looks different from the others, itches, bleeds, or is changing in any way, a dermatologist should examine it.
sources.conclusion: Supports clinician review for new, changing, bleeding, itching, painful, or otherwise concerning moles and no home remedies.
sources.uncertainty: Urgency still depends on clinical context.

sources.title: Warning Letter: Ariella Naturals
sources.exact_excerpt: There are no over-the-counter (OTC) drugs that can be legally sold for mole or skin tag removal
sources.conclusion: Supports prohibiting OTC mole-removal matching because no lawful OTC mole-removal drug exists and self-treatment may delay cancer diagnosis or cause injury.
sources.uncertainty: A warning letter is product-specific enforcement evidence, used here only for the stated regulatory and safety principle.

sources.title: FDA warns against purchasing or using chemical peel skin products without professional supervision
sources.exact_excerpt: The agency has not approved any chemical peel products
sources.conclusion: Supports prohibiting unsupervised high-strength chemical peels and describing burns, infection, pigment change, and scarring as harms.
sources.uncertainty: Does not compare supervised procedures or establish which supervised procedure is effective.

sources.title: Skin Cancer: Screening
sources.recommendation_strength: I statement: insufficient evidence for clinician visual screening in asymptomatic adolescents and adults.
sources.exact_excerpt: The USPSTF concludes that the current evidence is insufficient to assess the balance of benefits and harms
sources.conclusion: Prevents the application from misrepresenting its nevus label as validated screening or using an asymptomatic-screening recommendation to reassure a symptomatic user.
sources.uncertainty: Does not apply to diagnosis of a symptomatic or changing lesion and does not evaluate this model.

rejected_source_classes.reason: Permitted only as a lead for product identity, never as clinical efficacy, safety, or exact-label support.
Excluded from clinical support; manufacturer evidence may later verify exact SKU ingredients, SPF, role, directions, and warnings only.
Could not be mapped to one exact detector label and auditable management conclusion.
Avoided selective citation and redundant source volume when a current guideline or systematic review supplied the needed conclusion.

rejected_sources.title: Differin (adapalene) gel 0.1% 2024 label PDF
rejected_sources.reason: Replaced by S04, a current DailyMed Drug Facts label updated 2026-06-01; the PDF itself warns that it may not be the latest approved labeling.

rejected_sources.title: Melasma Management: A Comprehensive Review of Treatments and Emerging Therapies
rejected_sources.reason: Professional feature rather than a formal clinical-practice guideline; relevant conclusions were already supported by S11 and S12.

rejected_sources.title: Efficacy of topical silicone gel in scar management: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomised controlled trials
rejected_sources.reason: Retained the directly applicable Cochrane review S10 as the higher-rigor synthesis and avoided double-counting overlapping small trials.

rejected_sources.title: Acne: Overview
rejected_sources.reason: Did not add a claim beyond the more specific morphology, differential-diagnosis, guideline, and regulatory sources S01-S07.

product_evidence_sources.exact_variant: Clinique Acne Solutions All-Over Clearing Treatment Oil-Free
product_evidence_sources.uncertainty: Catalog evidence approval is separate from the AI research audit; the synthetic MVP path is approved while completeness remains partial.

product_evidence_sources.exact_variant: Supergoop! Glowscreen Sunscreen SPF 40 PA+++ with Hyaluronic Acid + Niacinamide
product_evidence_sources.uncertainty: Iron-oxide percentage is not stated; completeness remains partial and real-user authorization is absent.

product_evidence_sources.exact_variant: Supergoop! (Re)setting 100% Mineral Powder Sunscreen SPF 35 PA+++
product_evidence_sources.uncertainty: None relevant to the gate: powder is a known incompatible primary sunscreen format and is therefore excluded.

product_evidence_sources.exact_variant: Supergoop! Mineral Mattescreen Sunscreen SPF 40 PA+++
product_evidence_sources.uncertainty: Iron-oxide percentage is not stated; completeness remains partial and real-user authorization is absent.

product_evidence_sources.exact_variant: Tatcha The Silk Sunscreen Mineral Broad Spectrum SPF 50 PA++++ with Hyaluronic Acid and Niacinamide
product_evidence_sources.uncertainty: Format is unknown and lowers completeness but does not veto under D-036; iron-oxide percentage is not stated; real-user authorization is absent.

product_coverage_audit.reproducibility_exception: Two derived catalog inputs are absent from clean commit 94045f8, and the committed verification overlay and concern-active file do not match the recorded hashes. Regenerate or pin these inputs before treating exact counts or SKU eligibility as fixed acceptance criteria.
product_coverage_audit.method: Load current full and drug catalogs, apply the approved verification overlay at the fixed audit timestamp, parse actives deterministically, and run current hard gates with a complete neutral coverage-audit profile. Presence counts are not eligibility claims.

silicone_scar_care.rule: Do not infer scar-care efficacy from generic vehicle silicones such as dimethicone.

closed_comedo.reason: No OTC adapalene or exact salicylic-acid acne-drug row clears the current treatment gates.

open_comedo.reason: No OTC adapalene or exact salicylic-acid acne-drug row clears the current treatment gates.

nodule.reason: Clinician-first; no retail target is allowed.

atrophic_scar.reason: Clinician-first and procedure discussion; no retail target is allowed.

hypertrophic_scar.reason: No dedicated, verified silicone sheet or gel product is present.

nevus.reason: Monitoring or referral only; no product target is allowed.

other.reason: Unsupported; no product target is allowed.

required_invariants: policy JSON parses and contains each exact label once
production_eligible is false, mvp_synthetic_eligible is true, and ai_research_audit.approved is true
test_only is true and any runtime translation is confined to tests/fixtures
authorized environments are development and test; authorized inputs are synthetic profiles and fixture images only
real-user, external-beta, and production use remain unauthorized
nodule, atrophic_scar, hypertrophic_scar, nevus, and other have no retail target active
nevus and other have no required product role
every included, rejected-with-snapshot, and product-evidence snapshot exists and hashes to snapshot_sha256
exact-label coverage names all 10 labels and preserves every unfilled path
product coverage counts and SKU eligibility are marked provisional until their dirty-worktree inputs are pinned or regenerated
report and policy hash to the values recorded in artifacts
